text: This paper examines the perceptions and attitudes of middle aged visitors towards agritourism and explores their distinctively structured set of visitor behaviours and expectations in the context of rural tourism. The paper discusses the push and pull factors that drive this particular age group towards selecting agritourism as their tourist activity and in their destination choice. It also examines the different communication channels through which tourists are informed of the various possibilities that can be encountered in rural areas, the holiday activities they aspire to carry out, their requests in terms of service diversification and the factors which guide their tourist behaviour. The results of this study give a first opportunity for the evaluation and improvement of the agritourism services provided to meet the growing demands of this new category of tourists.
